summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of the legendary July 3 1988 Oxford Plains show – soaring Bird Song, a smoking Hey Pocky Way and tight playing from the whole band.
review: The recording is an excellent SBD source; reviewers rave about its crispness, deep bass extension and lack of the usual “canned” feel. The performance itself is remembered for the ultra‑light over Bird Song, a blistering Hey Pocky Way that launches a second set packed with Queen Jane, Touch of Grey, I Will Take You Home and an inspired jam flow. Minor quirks (a brief hiss on Sugaree, a loose Estimated) do not detract from the overall high‑energy, fan‑favorite show.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews (majority 5‑star) praise both musical performance and recording fidelity; only a handful of minor complaints keep the score just below perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear mix with strong bass extension; occasional brief hiss on Sugaree, slight bass ‘unruliness’ on some systems and one note of tinny pitch‑shift on a particular SBD copy.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A short, punchy first set leads into a powerhouse second set that opens with Hey Pocky Way, rides through tight jams (Touch, Eyes) and finishes with an emotional Dear Mr. Fantasy → Hey Jude finale; overall pacing is praised as energetic and well‑balanced.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as strong and excited (especially on Dear Mr. Fantasy); guitar tone noted as good, and he’s seen smiling at the glider during Bird Song.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass repeatedly called ‘thundering’, with excellent extension and clean presence; some listeners note a heavy mix but generally love his playing.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s pink Telecaster/Modulus Pepto gets mixed mentions – praised for energy, occasional roughness on vocals and guitar level changes noted by tape‑makers.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland dominates the praise: ‘big Brent night’, superb Hammond work on Walking Blues, standout piano solo in Eyes of the World, and driving Hey Pocky Way performance.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey & Bill (Rhythm Devils) get kudos for a tight pre‑drums/space segment and an energetic drum circle; one reviewer calls it ‘muscular’.
